channel.title: Earnings
channel.description: Corporate Company Earnings, Find Earnings Per Share and Earnings History Online

item.title: Domino's Pizza stock falls on disappointing sales — and CEO thinks more chains will follow
item.description: CEO Russell Weiner said he expects more fast-food chains will report that winter weather and weak consumer sentiment hurt their quarterly sales.

item.title: P&G's solid quarter, confident outlook proves why the stock deserves a spot in our portfolio
item.description: P&G was able to do this despite increased commodity prices due to the Iran war.

item.title: Procter & Gamble earnings beat estimates as sales grow 7%
item.description: The company reiterated its full-year forecast for earnings and sales.

item.title: Intel's stock soars 20% as results top estimates, with chipmaker showing signs of growth
item.description: Intel has been a Wall Street darling of late even as the business has yet to find much momentum.

item.title: Honeywell disappoints on quarterly results — but delivers on its breakup plan
item.description: Investors focused solely on the numbers are missing the forest for the trees.

item.title: Overlooked industrial Dover gets the market's attention with a strong first quarter
item.description: We're raising our price target on the industrial conglomerate, which makes everything from gas pumps to data center components.

item.title: American Airlines cuts 2026 earnings projections after surge in jet fuel
item.description: American Airlines cut its 2026 earnings forecast, becoming the latest airline to lower its outlook after a surge in fuel costs added billions to its expenses.

item.title: Comcast beats revenue, earnings expectations as broadband losses improve
item.description: Comcast’s first-quarter earnings were lifted by NBC’s sports slate in February as well as fewer broadband customer losses than prior quarters.

item.title: L'Oreal stock pops 9% after 'impressive' earnings, notches biggest gain in 18 years
item.description: The cosmetics market shows no signs of slowing down, analysts said after L'Oreal's "impressive" results.

item.title: SK Hynix posts record first-quarter profit, in line with estimates as memory prices climb
item.description: SK Hynix posted yet another quarter of record profit and revenue, as prices for its products continue to surge amid strong AI demand.

item.title: IBM CEO Krishna says Iran, other uncertainty is weighing on company's outlook
item.description: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna said he expects others to quickly catch up to the power and performance of Anthropic's Mythos AI model.

item.title: Southwest Airlines forecasts quarterly earnings below estimates on higher fuel
item.description: The airline reported that revenue in its first-quarter period rose nearly 13% to $7.25 billion.

item.title: ServiceNow stock sinks 14% as subscription revenue takes hit from Iran war
item.description: The software company beat expectations for earnings and revenue as it continued to expand its artificial intelligence offerings.

item.title: IBM shares drop as company beats but opts to maintain guidance
item.description: Investors have been attuned to mainframe disruption threats from artificial intelligence, but IBM posted 51% growth in Z mainframe hardware revenue.

item.title: Tesla misses on revenue but beats on profit as auto margins jump
item.description: Tesla's stock has underperformed all of its megacap peers so far this year as global competition ramps up in the electric vehicle market.

item.title: Chubb's earnings blew past the Street. Here's why the stock is falling
item.description: Instead of focusing on those numbers, investors are spooked by signs of a softening property insurance market, with competition increasing and rates declining.

item.title: We're raising our price target on GE Vernova as AI fuels another monster quarter
item.description: All AI roads lead back to energy demand, and that puts GE Vernova on the ground floor of the Fourth Industrial Revolution.

item.title: Boeing takes a step forward in its turnaround. Here's what to watch for next
item.description: The planemaker reiterated its full-year cash flow guidance and continues to makes progress on long-delayed plane deliveries.

item.title: Boeing CEO says 'all systems are go' to increase 737 production as company narrows loss
item.description: Boeing expects certification of new Max 7 and 10 models later this year, with deliveries starting in 2027.

item.title: Capital One shares slide after a double miss. Here's why we're staying the course
item.description: There are some puts and takes that explain why the stock isn't being punished more for the double miss.

item.title: United Airlines slashes 2026 forecast as fuel costs surge, but demand remains strong
item.description: The carrier's first-quarter earnings topped Wall Street's expectations.

item.title: Stocks give up their gains — plus, an early trend we're watching this earnings season
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: UnitedHealth tops quarterly estimates, hikes profit outlook as insurer manages high medical costs
item.description: The nation's largest private insurer expects 2026 adjusted earnings of more than $18.25 per share, up from a previous outlook of greater than $17.75 per share.

item.title: Netflix was long 'a builder not a buyer.' Is that era over?
item.description: Netflix co-CEO Ted Sarandos said during an investor call that the company built its "M&A muscle" during its pursuit of WBD's assets.

item.title: Netflix stock sinks after streamer reiterates guidance, says Reed Hastings to exit board
item.description: Netflix beat expectations for Q1 revenue and reported a big jump in earnings per share thanks in part to a termination fee related to its proposed WBD deal.

item.title: World's biggest chocolate maker issues profit warning as cocoa prices collapse; shares plunge 17%
item.description: Swiss chocolate maker Barry Callebaut on Thursday lowered its operating profit outlook, citing supply concerns and industry overcapacity.

item.title: PepsiCo earnings beat estimates as Doritos, Lay's price cuts win back shoppers
item.description: PepsiCo's quarterly earnings and revenue topped Wall Street's expectations.

item.title: EasyJet's stock drops 5% as the Middle East conflict, fuel costs weigh on bookings outlook
item.description: EasyJet said it took on roughly £25 million in additional fuel costs in the first half of 2026 due to the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Kering looks to double profits as it unveils ambitious turnaround plan to revive Gucci
item.description: The "ReconKering" strategy announced Thursday includes more than doubling its 2025 recurring operating margin of 11.1%.

item.title: TSMC first-quarter profit rises 58%, beats estimates as AI demand fuels record run
item.description: TSMC reported another quarter of record profit, with the company expecting AI demand to continue to grow.
